question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: setBloom(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction carrying a maximum-size bloom byte array (e.g., far exceeding 256 bytes) so that Arrays.equals(bloom, EMPTY_BLOOM) performs a full comparison on every ingestion, exhausting CPU across sustained high-throughput block processing and causing the importer to stall, denying service to the entire mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: setBloom(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user craft transactions whose bloom field is exactly equal to EMPTY_BLOOM (new byte[256]) at scale, forcing repeated Arrays.equals comparisons and ArrayUtils.EMPTY_BYTE_ARRAY assignments in a tight loop, causing GC pressure and JVM heap exhaustion in the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NullPointerException-based crash loop by submitting a contract transaction where payerAccountId resolves to null, causing payerAccountId.getId() in toContractTransactionHash() to throw an unchecked NPE that propagates up and halts the record parsing pipeline, resulting in a non-network DoS of the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a null transactionHash field so that toContractTransactionHash() builds a ContractTransactionHash with a null hash (the @Id field), causing a constraint violation or null-key collision in the database that crashes the batch insert pipeline and halts all downstream contract result processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isNew() always returns true to force Hibernate to always attempt an INSERT (never a merge/update), so that submitting duplicate consensusTimestamp values across transactions causes repeated primary key constraint violations, filling the database error log and causing the batch persister to throw ParserException in a loop, halting the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a high volume of contract transactions with colliding consensusTimestamp values, and because isNew() unconditionally returns true (bypassing Hibernate's existence check), cause the batch insert to fail with duplicate key exceptions on every batch, effectively blocking all contract_result writes and causing a sustained DoS of the importer's persistence layer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Field: callResult] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction with a maximally large callResult byte array (e.g., megabytes of return data), causing the importer to allocate a large heap object per ContractResult instance, and when batched together exhaust JVM heap memory, triggering OutOfMemoryError and crashing the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Field: failedInitcode] Can an unprivileged external user deploy a contract with a maximally large failedInitcode byte array (e.g., the maximum EVM initcode size), so that every failed contract creation stores a large blob in the failedInitcode field, causing the database to grow unboundedly and disk I/O to saturate, resulting in a non-network DoS of the mirror node's storage layer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Field: functionParameters] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with a maximally large functionParameters byte array, causing each ContractResult object to hold a large byte[] on the heap, and when the batch persister accumulates many such objects before flushing, exhaust JVM heap memory and crash the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Field: functionResult] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'temporary field' nature of functionResult (which stores functionResult.toByteArray() — the full serialized ContractFunctionResult proto) by submitting contract calls with maximally large function results, causing double storage of data (both in callResult and functionResult), doubling database write amplification and causing disk exhaustion?
